checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly converts the slide-based presentation into a single-column scrolling format. The logical flow of the information matches the source PDF. The conversion preserves the relationship between headings and their subsequent content blocks (bullets, tables).

The tables in the HTML (e.g., the 'Improvement from Stop Consolidation' table on page 16 or the 'Here's your tally...' data on page 17) are rendered correctly with legible rows and columns. The conversion also successfully transformed form-like layouts (e.g., the student data entry forms on page 9) and report structures (e.g., bus routing information on page 11) into standard HTML tables that maintain data alignment and readability.

Numerous meaningful images from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. This includes complex diagrams (the TIMS system on page 8, which is replaced by a green box), photos (bus near railroad tracks on page 11), charts (Number of Buses Operated line graph on page 19), and, most critically, nearly all GIS maps (pages 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, 22, and 23). Since the document's subject is 'The Value of GIS', the absence of these maps significantly undermines the visual fidelity and utility of the conversion.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains critical images: Page 8 (TIMS diagram), Page 11 (bus/railroad photo), Page 12 (RR Crossing map), Page 13 (Optimization map), Page 14/15 (Case Study maps), Page 17 (Boundary map), Page 19 (Buses operated chart), Page 22 (Income map), and Page 23 (Growth map).
checks.b_evidence_output: These meaningful maps, photos, and charts are absent from the HTML rendering or replaced by placeholders (e.g., the green square on page 8).
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically under headings corresponding to PDF pages 8, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, 19, 22, and 23.

checks.a_analysis: The content of the final slide (page 24) is a duplicate of the title slide (page 1) in the source PDF. The HTML output accurately reflects this duplication found in the source material. No other instances of genuine body content duplication were observed.
